title: Authors and Citation
text: Ella Kaye. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kaye E (2026). jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation Visualisation. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/EllaKaye/jugglr.
code: @Manual{,   title = {jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation},   author = {Ella Kaye},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/EllaKaye/jugglr}, }

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: jugglr R package validating visualising juggling patterns expressed siteswap notation. siteswap() function takes siteswap sequence creates S7 object, child classes vanilla, synchronous, multiplex, synchronous multiplex, passing siteswap.

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: can install development version jugglr GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("EllaKaye/jugglr")

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: Siteswap object created, print method display information sequence, whether valid juggling pattern , , many props uses.
code: library(jugglr) # A valid juggling pattern ss423 <- siteswap("423") ss423 #> ✔ '423' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3 # A pattern that cannot be juggled ss21 <- siteswap("21") ss21 #> ✖ '21' is not a valid juggling pattern #> ℹ The throws don't average to a whole number #> ℹ Two or more throws land on the same beat (collision)

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: three ways visualising siteswap patterns: timeline plot, shows throws beat ladder diagram, shows throws beat hand animation (pattern valid)

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: functions take Siteswap objects argument return ggplot2 plots, can customised.   plots also useful understanding non-valid sequences jugglable. can see, example, two props need caught time (permissible vanilla siteswap). prop shown different colour, can see balls disappearing needing suddenly appear.
code: timeline(ss423) ladder(ss423) timeline(ss21) ladder(ss21)

title: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation
text: jugglr provides wrapper JugglingLab GIF server. Unlike plotting functions, main argument valid siteswap sequence string. called Positron RStudio, animate() show animation Viewer pane, otherwise browser. path argument supplied, animation saved location instead. Note can take several seconds animation render. embedding RMarkdown quarto documents, wrapper, animate_markdown(), calls knitr::include_graphics(), display options can set chunk arguments (e.g. output , .width="40%"):
code: animate_markdown(   "423",    path = "man/figures/423-animation.gif",    colors = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73")   ) #> ✔ Animation saved to: 'man/figures/423-animation.gif'

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
text: Creates typed siteswap object detecting notation style sequence. Vanilla siteswaps (alphanumeric strings "531") produce vanillaSiteswap object; synchronous siteswaps (,b) notation "(4,2x)*" produce synchronousSiteswap object; multiplex siteswaps square-bracket groups "[43]1" produce multiplexSiteswap object; passing siteswaps <|B> notation "<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>" produce passingSiteswap object.

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
code: siteswap(sequence)

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
text: sequence single character string siteswap notation.

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
text: vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, passingSiteswap S7 object.

title: Create a siteswap object — siteswap
code: siteswap("531") #> ✔ '531' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3 siteswap("(4,2x)*") #> ✔ '(4,2x)*' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,2x)(2x,4) #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4 siteswap("[43]1") #> ✔ '[43]1' is valid multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2 siteswap("(2,4)([4x4],2x)") #> ✔ '(2,4)([4x4],2x)' is valid synchronous multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 4 siteswap("<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>") #> ✔ '<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 6

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: Generates animated GIF juggling pattern via JugglingLab GIF server displays RStudio viewer (default browser). path given GIF saved disk instead.

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
code: animate(   pattern,   colors = NULL,   prop = NULL,   bps = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   fps = NULL,   slowdown = NULL,   ...,   path = NULL )  animate_markdown(pattern, path, colors = NULL, prop = NULL, ...)

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: pattern siteswap pattern string (e.g. "531") siteswap object: vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap. passingSiteswap objects also accepted sequence format (<|B>) valid JugglingLab notation. colors Optional. vector R colours (one per prop), one special strings "mixed" "orbits". Passed JugglingLab. prop Prop type: "ball" (default), "ring", "image". bps Beats per second (numeric scalar). Controls animation speed. width, height Width height animation pixels (numeric scalars). fps Frames per second (numeric scalar). slowdown Slowdown factor (numeric scalar). JugglingLab default 2.0; values greater slow animation , values less 2.0 speed . ... Additional named arguments passed JugglingLab GIF server. Pattern-setting arguments include dwell, hands, body, propdiam, gravity, bouncefrac, squeezebeats, hss, handspec, dwellmax, hold. Animation arguments include stereo, border, camangle, showground, hidejugglers. values must scalars. path Path save GIF (must end .gif). NULL (default) animation displayed viewer.

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: Invisibly returns path temporary HTML file (displaying) save path (path given). knitr graphics object inline embedding.

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: Note setting colors can introduce short delay server renders animation.

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
text: animate_markdown(): Save GIF path embed R Markdown Quarto document via knitr::include_graphics(). path argument required; set persistent file path (tempfile()) rendered document can reference . arguments accepted animate() (prop, bps, slowdown, width, height, ... arguments) can passed .

title: Animate a juggling pattern — animate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ animate("531") animate(vanillaSiteswap("531"), prop = "ring", bps = 5) animate("531", path = tempfile(fileext = ".gif")) } # }  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In an R Markdown or Quarto document: animate_markdown("531", path = "531.gif") } # }

what: jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation — jugglr-package
title: jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation — jugglr-package
text: Validate visualise juggling patterns expressed siteswap notation. Supports vanilla, synchronous, multiplex, synchronous multiplex, passing siteswap, tools check pattern validity, produce timeline arc diagrams ladder diagrams, retrieve raw throw data custom visualisations, animate patterns via JugglingLab GIF server.

title: jugglr: Juggling Pattern Validation and Visualisation — jugglr-package
text: Maintainer: Ella Kaye ella.kaye@gmail.com [copyright holder] Authors: Ella Kaye ella.kaye@gmail.com [copyright holder]

what: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
text: Produces ladder diagram showing prop paths hands across beats. Straight lines indicate self-throws (hand catches); crossing lines indicate cross-throws (opposite hand catches).

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
code: ladder(   siteswap,   n_cycles = 3,   direction = c("horizontal", "vertical", "h", "v"),   title = TRUE,   subtitle = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
text: siteswap vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap object. n_cycles Number complete cycles simulate (default 3). direction Orientation diagram: "horizontal" (default, time runs left right) "vertical" (time runs top bottom). Shorthands "h" "v" also accepted. title Logical. TRUE (default), adds title showing siteswap sequence. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. subtitle Logical. TRUE (default), adds subtitle showing siteswap type number props. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot a ladder diagram for a siteswap — ladder
code: s <- vanillaSiteswap("531") ladder(s)

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: Creates multiplex siteswap object sequence square brackets group simultaneous throws hand. example, "[43]1" means juggler throws heights 4 3 simultaneously first beat, single 1 second beat. slot without brackets single throw exactly vanilla siteswap.

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
code: multiplexSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: sequence single character string multiplex siteswap notation, e.g. "[43]1" "[33]".

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: multiplexSiteswap S7 object.

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
text: @type Always "multiplex" (read-). @slots List integer vectors, one element per time slot. Single-throw slots length-1 vectors; multiplex slots length > 1. @throws Integer vector individual throw heights (flattened across slots). @period Number time slots per cycle. @symmetry "symmetrical" period odd; "asymmetrical" period even. @n_props Total throw heights divided period — number props required. (Note: sum(throws) / period, mean(throws).) @can_throw TRUE every slot number props thrown equals number props landing (conservation violation). @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Multiplex siteswap — multiplexSiteswap
code: multiplexSiteswap("[43]1") #> ✔ '[43]1' is valid multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2 multiplexSiteswap("[33]") #> ✔ '[33]' is valid multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 1  s <- multiplexSiteswap("[43]1") s@n_props #> [1] 4 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: Creates passing siteswap object multi-juggler sequence <|B> notation. section separated | gives one juggler's throws; p suffix (e.g. "3p") marks throw passes next juggler. Fractional notation (e.g. "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>") also supported, .5 throws passes second juggler half beat later.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
code: passingSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: sequence single character string passing siteswap notation, e.g. "<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>" "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>". Spaces within sections ignored; <3p33|3p33> <3p 3 3|3p 3 3> equivalent.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: passingSiteswap S7 object.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: p-notation, jugglers fully synchronised: throw beat alternate hands together. fractional notation, second juggler offset half beat combined timeline.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
text: @type Always "passing" (read-). @is_fractional TRUE fractional notation; FALSE p-notation. @n_jugglers Number jugglers (sections sequence). @sequences_by_juggler List character vectors, one per juggler, giving raw tokens (e.g. c("3p", "3", "3")). @throws_by_juggler List numeric vectors, one per juggler, throw heights (integers p-notation; may include .5 fractional). @is_pass_by_juggler List logical vectors, one per juggler, indicating throws passes. @period Number throws per juggler per cycle. @symmetry "symmetrical" period odd; "asymmetrical" period even. @n_props Total number props (sum(throws) / period). @can_throw TRUE two throws land (juggler, beat, hand) triple (collision). @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Passing siteswap — passingSiteswap
code: passingSiteswap("<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>") #> ✔ '<3p 3 3 3 3 3 | 3p 3 3 3 3 3>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 6 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 6 passingSiteswap("<4p 3 | 3 4p>") #> ✔ '<4p 3 | 3 4p>' is valid passing siteswap #> ℹ It uses 7 props across 2 jugglers #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 2  # Compact and spaced forms are equivalent s1 <- passingSiteswap("<3p33|3p33>") s2 <- passingSiteswap("<3p 3 3 | 3p 3 3>") identical(s1@throws_by_juggler, s2@throws_by_juggler) #> [1] TRUE  s <- passingSiteswap("<4p 3 | 3 4p>") s@n_props #> [1] 7 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: Creates synchronous multiplex siteswap object. combines synchronous notation (hands throw simultaneously, written pairs parentheses) multiplex notation (square brackets group simultaneous throws hand). example, "(2,4)([4x4],2x)" 4-prop pattern second slot hand 0 throwing two balls simultaneously.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
code: synchronousMultiplexSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: sequence single character string synchronous multiplex siteswap notation, e.g. "(2,4)([4x4],2x)".

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: synchronousMultiplexSiteswap S7 object.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: individual throw heights must even. x suffix marks crossing throw; trailing * indicates pattern alternates two mirrored versions.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
text: @type Always "synchronous multiplex" (read-). @full_sequence expanded sequence * shorthand resolved. @throws Character vector slot throw strings across one expanded cycle. Multiplex groups kept single elements, e.g. "[4x4]". @throws_by_hand Named list elements hand_1 hand_2, character vector throw strings per slot (one per sync slot). @period Number throw slots per full cycle (2 × number sync slots, since hands throw every beat; always even). @symmetry "symmetrical" pattern mirror image; "asymmetrical" otherwise. @n_props Number props: sum throw heights divided period. @can_throw TRUE thrown landing prop counts balance every (slot, hand) pair within one cycle. @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Synchronous multiplex siteswap — synchronousMultiplexSiteswap
code: synchronousMultiplexSiteswap("(2,4)([4x4],2x)") #> ✔ '(2,4)([4x4],2x)' is valid synchronous multiplex siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is asymmetrical with period 4 synchronousMultiplexSiteswap("(4,[42x])*") #> ✔ '(4,[42x])*' is valid synchronous multiplex siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,[42x])([42x],4) #> ℹ It uses 5 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4  s <- synchronousMultiplexSiteswap("(2,4)([4x4],2x)") s@n_props #> [1] 4 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: Creates synchronous siteswap object two-handed simultaneous-throw sequence. Synchronous notation describes patterns hands throw time, written pairs throw heights parentheses "(4,4)" "(4,2x)*". x suffix marks crossing throw; trailing * indicates pattern alternates two mirrored versions.

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
code: synchronousSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: sequence single character string synchronous siteswap notation, e.g. "(4,4)" "(4,2x)*".

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: synchronousSiteswap S7 object.

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
text: @type Always "synchronous" (read-). @full_sequence expanded sequence * shorthand resolved. @throws Character vector individual throw values across one expanded cycle. @throws_by_hand Named list elements hand_1 hand_2, character vector throws hand per slot. @period Number throw slots per full cycle (counts hands per simultaneous beat, always even). @symmetry "symmetrical" pattern mirror image; "asymmetrical" otherwise. @n_props Mean slide sequence, equal number props. @can_throw TRUE collisions occur slide sequence. @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Synchronous siteswap — synchronousSiteswap
code: synchronousSiteswap("(4,4)") #> ✔ '(4,4)' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ It uses 4 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 2 synchronousSiteswap("(4,2x)*") #> ✔ '(4,2x)*' is valid synchronous siteswap #> ℹ Full sequence: (4,2x)(2x,4) #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 4  s <- synchronousSiteswap("(4,2x)*") s@n_props #> [1] 3 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

what: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
text: Returns data frame describing throw simulated pattern, including beat hand throw originates , lands, prop thrown. underpins timeline() ladder(), can used directly custom visualisations.

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
code: throw_data(siteswap, n_cycles = 3, ...)

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
text: siteswap vanillaSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap object. n_cycles Number complete cycles simulate (default 3). Increase patterns many props ensure appear data. Setting n_cycles >= period * n_props * 2 guarantees prop thrown least twice. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data
text: data frame one row per throw columns: beat: throw beat index hand: throwing hand (0 1) throw: throw height (integer) catch_beat: beat prop lands catch_hand: hand catches (0 1) prop: prop identifier (integer)

title: Get throw data for a siteswap — throw_data

what: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: Produces timeline (arc) diagram showing trajectory prop across beats. arc represents throw, coloured prop. x-axis labels show throw heights siteswap sequence.

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
code: timeline(siteswap, n_cycles = 3, title = TRUE, subtitle = TRUE, ...)

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: siteswap vanillaSiteswap, multiplexSiteswap, synchronousSiteswap, synchronousMultiplexSiteswap object. n_cycles Number complete cycles simulate (default 3). warning issued props appear within simulated range. title Logical. TRUE (default), adds title showing siteswap sequence. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. subtitle Logical. TRUE (default), adds subtitle showing siteswap type number props. Set FALSE suppress; override ggplot2::labs() returned plot. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot a timeline diagram for a siteswap — timeline
code: s <- vanillaSiteswap("531") timeline(s)

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: Creates vanilla siteswap object alphanumeric siteswap sequence "531" "97531". Vanilla siteswap describes solo juggling patterns one prop thrown per beat, alternating hands.

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
code: vanillaSiteswap(sequence = character(0))

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: sequence single character string vanilla siteswap notation (digits letters , e.g. "531").

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: vanillaSiteswap S7 object.

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
text: @type Always "vanilla" (read-). @throws Integer vector throw heights one cycle. @period Number throws per cycle (length throws). @symmetry "symmetrical" period odd (pattern repeats swapped hands); "asymmetrical" period even. @n_props Mean throw height, equal number props required. @can_throw TRUE two throws land beat (collisions). @satisfies_average_theorem TRUE n_props whole number. @valid TRUE can_throw satisfies_average_theorem TRUE.

title: Vanilla siteswap — vanillaSiteswap
code: vanillaSiteswap("531") #> ✔ '531' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 3 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 3 vanillaSiteswap("97531") #> ✔ '97531' is valid vanilla siteswap #> ℹ It uses 5 props #> ℹ It is symmetrical with period 5  s <- vanillaSiteswap("531") s@n_props #> [1] 3 s@valid #> [1] TRUE

title: jugglr 0.0.0.9000
text: ladder() passingSiteswap now uses sequence plot title directly, consistent siteswap types. synchronousSiteswap throws_by_hand property declaration corrected class_character class_list match list returned get_sync_hands(). ladder() timeline() titles now show siteswap sequence directly (e.g. 531) rather prefixing "Siteswap '". synchronousSiteswap synchronousMultiplexSiteswap, timeline() now uses full_sequence (e.g. (4,2x)(4,2x)) match existing ladder() behaviour. ladder() subtitle--plot spacing now direction-aware: horizontal vertical ladder diagrams use different margins reduce excess whitespace prevent subtitle overlap. ladder() direction argument now also accepts "h" "v" shorthands "horizontal" "vertical". ladder() gains title argument (default TRUE) control whether plot title shown, matching existing subtitle argument. timeline() gains subtitle argument (default TRUE) control whether plot subtitle shown independently title argument. timeline() passingSiteswap now shows J1’s throws x-axis labels, J2 (jugglers’) throws displayed -plot text annotations juggler’s lane. timeline() synchronousSiteswap synchronousMultiplexSiteswap now displays L/R hand throws separate lines axis labels, reducing horizontal crowding. animate() animate_markdown() animate juggling patterns via JugglingLab GIF server. animate() gains prop argument selecting prop type ("ball", "ring", "image"). animate() @param pattern documentation updated list five siteswap types, noting passingSiteswap sequences valid JugglingLab notation. animate() animate_markdown() now accept [vanillaSiteswap], [synchronousSiteswap], [multiplexSiteswap] objects pattern, addition plain strings. animate_markdown() now exported. ladder() now works passingSiteswap objects, drawing multi-row diagram passes shown diagonal segments juggler rows. ladder() plots ladder diagrams vanilla, synchronous, multiplex siteswaps. multiplexSiteswap() creates multiplex siteswap S7 object patterns simultaneous throws one hand, written using square-bracket notation "[43]1". passingSiteswap() creates passing siteswap S7 object multi-juggler patterns <|B> notation. Supports p-notation (e.g. "<4p 3 | 3 4p>") fractional notation (e.g. "<4.5 3 3 | 3 4 3.5>"). Includes throw_data(), timeline(), ladder() methods. siteswap() now also dispatches passingSiteswap() <…|…> notation. siteswap() creates vanilla, synchronous, multiplex, synchronous multiplex siteswap object notation string. synchronousMultiplexSiteswap() creates synchronous multiplex siteswap S7 object patterns combining simultaneous two-handed throws multiplex groups, "(2,4)([4x4],2x)". synchronousSiteswap() creates synchronous siteswap S7 object. throw_data() returns raw throw data frame underlying visualisation functions, use custom visualisations. timeline() now works passingSiteswap objects, drawing multi-lane arc diagram passes shown arcs connecting juggler lanes. timeline() now works synchronousSiteswap objects. timeline() plots arc/timeline diagrams vanilla multiplex siteswaps. vanillaSiteswap() creates vanilla siteswap S7 object.
